subject: [For Review] Anbo Warehouse Commission and Choice SLS discount Proposal
deck_filename: Anbo Commission Charge and Impact to Choice Seller P&L_20250516 .pdf

content.answer:
Hi Ian and Jianghong,

Pls kindly receive the updated documents for Chris/Terence meeting, and the
content has been reviewed and aligned with Jianghong and Ning respectively~

Key changes vs Tuesday version:

   - Choice true cost savings to SLS has been adjusted to* rate reduction
   impact to overall CB with Choice volume*
   - Other adjustments: listed in deck using blue box

Confirmations on presenting pace: to align on if following is okay

   - Next Tuesday: we walk through with Terence first before goes to Chris
   ( in CB monthly management meeting)
   - Next Wednesday: we walkthrough with Chris (in Choice Updates to Chris
   meeting)

Best,

*Faye Yan*

Shopee | Cross Border

*Mobile:* +65 87767030

*Address:* 5 Science Park Drive, Shopee Building, Singapore 118265

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 8 – 9

   - So there is no volume discount that SLS benefits from because of
   SCS/Lovito?
- Even for BR with 30% of volume?

I find it quite hard to believe



Slide 10

   - Do not quite understand this view.
   - I need a view that shows seller P&L and then another line item that
   says seller P&L without SLS discount
   - And I thought we already broke even at seller P&L level in Apr/May?

Slide 11

   - I thought we should still get NN WH SLS discounts?

Especially if we
   offer the same to other sellers?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Pls find answers to your questions.

Slide 8 – 9

   -

   So there is no volume discount that SLS benefits from because of
   SCS/Lovito?
   -

   Even for BR with 30% of volume? I find it quite hard to believe

*>> SEA and BR markets reasons are different*

   -

   *SEA mkt  key reason ( @James Li [SZ] <james.li@shopee.com> **pls add
   on) : SLS volume is the cornerstone for the 3PL’s charters, so we have a
   strong control power during the negotiation to not increase too much
   without Choice volume *
   -

   *BR key reason ( @Ruofei Wang <phyllis.wang@shopee.com>  pls add on) :
   BRCB linehaul is resource constraint routes, adding some statics points
   from SLS side about the supply shortage facts*
   -

      *Shopee’s Volume 20% in VCP airport, 80% in GIG airport due to
      Customs Clearance warehouse location*
      -

      *However, VCP and GIG can only handle 9k Tons and 5k Tons, compared
      with GRU airport handles 24.6k Tons (Apr’25). This indicates a supply
      disparity*

Slide 10

   -

   Do not quite understand this view.
   -

   I need a view that shows seller P&L and then another line item that says
   seller P&L without SLS discount
   -

   And I thought we already broke even at seller P&L level in Apr/May?

*>> For the seller P&L explanation: pls find details key assumptions*

   -

   *For version #1 , it reflects the MP Anbo commission rate reduction but
   not reflect the SLS new “true cost saving” discount, this is to avoid
   Seagroup reported PnL too volatile *
   -

   *For version #2, reflect both MP anbo commission rate reduction and SLS
   3% "true cost savings“ discount rate*
   -

   *Added version 3, if you are referring to seller P&L without any SLS
   discount , that will bring seller PnL to be -33mil*

*May I kindly check, seller PnL with and without SLS discount, you are
referring to is a combination of version 1 and 2, or version 2 and 3

content.question:
Hi Ian,



Pls find answers to your questions.

Slide 8 – 9

   - So there is no volume discount that SLS benefits from because of
   SCS/Lovito?
- Even for BR with 30% of volume?

I find it quite hard to believe

*>> SEA and BR markets reasons are different*

   - *SEA mkt  key reason (  @James Li [SZ] <james.li@shopee.com> pls add
   on) **: SLS volume is the cornerstone for the 3PL’s charters, so we have
   a strong control power during the negotiation to not increase too much
   without Choice volume *
   - *BR key reason ( @Ruofei Wang <phyllis.wang@shopee.com>  pls add on) **:
   BRCB linehaul is resource constraint routes, adding some statics points
   from SLS side about the supply shortage facts*


   - *Shopee’s Volume 20% in VCP airport, 80% in GIG airport due to Customs
      Clearance warehouse location*
      - *However, VCP and GIG can only handle 9k Tons and 5k Tons, compared
      with GRU airport handles 24.6k Tons (Apr’25). This indicates a supply
      disparity*





Do not quite understand. @Jianghong Liu <jianghong.liu@shopee.com> maybe
can look to see what is fair here?

10% of SEA markets and 30% of BR volume
but no advantages in pricing really does not seem right.



Slide 10

   - Do not quite understand this view.
   - I need a view that shows seller P&L and then another line item that
   says seller P&L without SLS discount
   - And I thought we already broke even at seller P&L level in Apr/May?

*>> For the seller P&L explanation: pls find details key assumptions*

   - *For version #1 , it reflects the MP Anbo commission rate reduction
   but **not reflect **the SLS new “true cost saving” discount, this is to
   avoid Seagroup reported PnL too volatile *
   - *For version #2, reflect both MP anbo commission rate reduction and
   SLS **3% **"true cost savings“ discount rate*
   - *Added version 3, if you are referring to seller P&L without** any **SLS
   discount , that will bring seller PnL to be -33mil*

*May I kindly check, seller PnL with and without SLS discount, you are
referring to is a combination of version 1 and 2, or version 2 and 3?

Deck
is showing version 1 and 2*



I need 2 versions

   - Version 1 >> Seller P&L based on previous comms structure
   - Version 2 >> Seller P&L based on Anbo comms structure. And we will add
   another line item that shows SLS rates that we have to now pay (including
   fair discounts that should be given)



*For seller P&L breakeven, **Chengchen Huo <chengchen.huo@shopee.com> feel
free to add on*

   - *For Apr Seller P&L**, latest Choice projected actuals is negative,
   Choice team is under closing Apr actuals by next Monday, we will refresh
   then, actually all versions I received from choice team in Apr for seller
   P&L EOM projection are negative (volatile from -0.1mil to -0.7mil),
   Chengchen Huo <chengchen.huo@shopee.com> can help share if Ian received any
   positive PnL reporting from your end*





This was not what was told to me?

- *For May Seller P&L**, latest Choice EOM projection is 23k
   (breakeven), however, this is too close and we choose to not update but use
   budget number*





We will use the optimistic version that we gave to CF before



Slide 11

   - I thought we should still get NN WH SLS discounts?

Especially if we
   offer the same to other sellers?

content.answer:
Hi Ian and Jianghong,

Post Today's Chris/Terence meeting, to confirm the next step for Anbo and
Choice SLS discount. Pls kindly see if this is aligned across, thanks!

   - *Base Commission adjustment*:
      - Agree to 2% discount vs CNCB 3P SLS sellers for all orders ship
      from Anbo warehouse, and will set in system as no transferring
pricing risk
      - *Specially highlight to Choice team,* for seller PnL, *1.8mil* FY25
      shall be reserved in seller P&L for the decrease in CB MP
revenue to ensure
      no impact to local platform P&L
   - *SLS Discount rate:*
      - Will *NOT *adjust in system, this is to 1) ensure SeaGroup level
      Choice seller P&L remain consistent, and 2) consult SeaFin on this, any
      changes to system setting of ASF adjustment will need to go through Group
      Tax as more profit flow in SG SLS entity may impact group tax planning
      - For CB internal tracking, Choice side to track seller P&L both
      current discount and with new SLS discount; CB FP&A will also
track seller
      P&L and SLS P&L both current and new SLS discount for reference

Pls kindly see if this is aligned across, thanks!

Best,

*Faye Yan*

Shopee | Cross Border

*Mobile:* +65 87767030

*Address:* 5 Science Park Drive, Shopee Building, Singapore 118265
